description: Drafting discipline rules: how to write the draft (length, punctuation, structure, openings, information delivery). All V2 rules use positive polarity.

rules.name: length-matched-to-substance
rules.directive: Match draft length to the substance of the guest's message. A short check-in confirmation gets a short reply. A multi-part question gets a draft long enough to cover each part.

rules.name: punctuation-discipline
rules.directive: Use commas, periods, parentheses, or colons for breaks between clauses. Em-dashes do not appear in drafts.

rules.name: prose-paragraphs-default
rules.directive: Default to prose paragraphs for guest-facing drafts. Bullets are reserved for procedural information where stepwise structure aids clarity (check-in instructions, multi-step troubleshooting).

rules.name: direct-hospitality-openings
rules.directive: Open guest-facing drafts with a short, natural hospitality greeting when the channel and thread cadence make it appropriate. Use the guest's first name when available. Then move directly into the substance of the guest's need. Skip empty filler and meta-commentary such as "I hope you are well", "thank you for your message", or "here is a draft" when it does not add value. In rapid WhatsApp-style back-and-forth, a greeting can be omitted if it would feel repetitive or unnatural. For email, include a greeting and a warm sign-off unless the thread is already in a terse operational exchange.

rules.name: inline-information-delivery
rules.directive: Deliver requested facts directly inline (passwords, codes, check-in times, addresses, distances). Do not delay or wrap requested facts in conditional or deferral phrasing when the team would simply state them.
